questions.question_text: You're building an agent with tools. Which approach will give Claude the most flexibility to handle unexpected requests?

options.text: Give Claude only one powerful tool
Provide very specific tools like "write_python_function" and "debug_code"
Provide abstract tools like "read_file", "write_file", and "run_command"
Provide tools that only work for planned scenarios

questions.question_text: You want Claude to write a report, then check if it's good enough, and improve it if needed. What pattern are you using?

options.text: Chaining workflow
Evaluator-Optimizer pattern
Parallelization workflow
Routing workflow

questions.question_text: Your app generates different types of social media content. Programming topics need educational scripts, while sports topics need entertainment-focused content. What pattern should you use?

options.text: Route requests to specialized processing pipelines
Use the same prompt for everything
Ask users to write their own content
Always use the entertainment approach

questions.question_text: Claude keeps ignoring some of your rules when you give it a long prompt with many requirements. What workflow approach would help?

options.text: Make the prompt even longer with more rules
Run everything in parallel
Use a routing workflow to categorize first
Chain the task into focused sequential steps

questions.question_text: You need Claude to recommend the best material for a part by considering metal, plastic, ceramic, and wood options. Each material has different criteria. What's the best approach?

options.text: Chain the evaluations one after another
Ask Claude to pick randomly
Send separate requests for each material type in parallel
Put all criteria in one big prompt

questions.question_text: You need to choose between a workflow and an agent for your app. Reliability and predictable results are most important to you. Which should you pick?

options.text: Always use an agent for maximum flexibility
Use a workflow since it's more reliable and testable
Combine both approaches equally
Use whichever is easier to code

questions.question_text: You're building an app where users upload photos of damaged car parts and always get repair cost estimates. You know exactly what steps are needed each time. What should you use?

options.text: Multiple agents working together
A single complex prompt
An agent with many specialized tools
A workflow with predetermined steps
